ground_truth.title: What is the tipping process in restaurants in the US?
ground_truth.author: US Tourist
ground_truth.main_content: The question asks about the tipping process in US restaurants: "I'm supposed to write down something on the bill I get? Do I hand the waiter my credit card after doing so? Since I need a receipt, do I wait for the waiter to bring things back? What's the process, from asking for the check till I can walk out with a receipt?" The accepted answer provides a step-by-step guide: 1) Get the bill from the server and check it for issues, 2) Give the server your credit card, 3) The server swipes your card and returns with your card and receipt, 4) There are two copies - write the tip on the restaurant copy and sign it, take your copy and card, 5) Leave the restaurant copy of the receipt on the table as you walk out. Another detailed answer explains that if paying by card, the restaurant will take your card away at the table (table-side wireless terminals are uncommon in the US), and you'll receive back your card, a pen, your receipt copy, and the restaurant's receipt with tip lines. You write the tip amount on the tip line, calculate the total, and sign. For cash payments, simply leave enough cash to cover the meal plus tip. Standard tipping is 15-20% of the bill. Some restaurants automatically add gratuity for large parties.
with: What is the tipping process in restaurants in the US?
Get the bill from the server. Check it for any issues. Give the server your credit card.
There are two copies of the receipt, one for you and one for the restaurant. Write the tip on the restaurant copy and sign it; take your copy and your card.
Leave the restaurant copy of the receipt on the table as you walk out of the restaurant.
If you're paying by card, place your credit card in the check-holder or hand it to the waiter. At most restaurants, they will take the card away from the table.
